behaviors.description: Outgoing API request always includes the full taxonomy label list.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module boundary (request builder -> API client payload handoff), not just a pure local algorithm. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; an in-process fake client can catch missing labels. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model judgment/output quality. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is only about request construction content, not live provider behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake provider client that records outbound payload. Invoke classify(doc, taxonomy) and assert every valid label appears in the sent request with no omissions across representative taxonomy sizes.

behaviors.description: Invalid provider label is mapped to "unknown".
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior depends on response handoff from provider adapter into normalization/validation logic. REAL-INFRA: real network is unnecessary; a stubbed provider response reproduces the failure. LLM-DEP: assertion is about local validation against fixed taxonomy, not model quality. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because disqualifying text is explicit: 'When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy' (fixed invalid fixture), so model decision quality is not under test.
behaviors.test_description: Use module with fake provider returning out-of-taxonomy labels (e.g., 'medical'). Assert module returns 'unknown' and never forwards raw invalid values.

behaviors.description: Normalization is deterministic for identical raw responses.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is explicitly one local normalization step; no cross-module interaction is required. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none, because input is identical raw response fixtures. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration+ because behavior text scopes to 'module's normalization step' determinism.
behaviors.test_description: Directly test normalization function/object with repeated identical raw response fixtures (case/whitespace variants already fixed per fixture). Assert same final label every run.

behaviors.description: Repeated live calls stay label-consistent unless provider response changes.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real AI API outputs across repeated calls; stubs would hide nondeterministic drift. REAL-INFRA also exists, but Agentic is the tighter boundary because model behavior is the risk. MULTI-STEP: repeated calls are not a chained multi-agent pipeline, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration due explicit 'live API calls' and consistency requirement tied to provider behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated real classifications for the same document+taxonomy against the live provider, capturing raw responses and final labels. Assert label stays stable when raw provider output is unchanged; flag instability attributable to provider/model variance.

behaviors.description: Token budget policy is enforced before dispatch (truncate/reject).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure occurs at coordination boundary between budget enforcement and dispatch path (must prevent/modify outbound request). REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with fake provider client; no real OS/network required. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output content. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is pre-dispatch policy enforcement, not live provider/model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Configure small token budget; run classify with over-budget input using fake provider transport. Assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) occurs and dispatch behavior matches policy (adjusted payload or no outbound call).

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage stays within configured budget for typical docs.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: behavior relies on real AI API/tokenization accounting; mocked usage can always be made to pass and hide real overruns. REAL-INFRA is also involved, but the minimum boundary is the real model/provider response. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because concrete text 'Provider-reported token usage' requires live provider semantics, not local deterministic stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real provider calls for a representative 'typical document' set, record provider-reported token usage metadata, and assert each call is <= configured per-call budget.

behaviors.description: On rate-limit signal, module waits and retries instead of failing immediately.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is orchestration/state handoff across retry logic and provider adapter, so above pure Unit. REAL-INFRA: real network/rate limiting is not strictly required; deterministic fake responses (rate-limit then success) can expose retry bugs. LLM-DEP: zero dependence on model output quality/content; this is infrastructure around the call. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network, (b) no real AI judgment needed, (c) no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic using behavior text 'API signals request rate is too high' (transport/control-path condition).
behaviors.test_description: Use fake provider that returns rate-limit error on first N attempts then success; verify module waits per backoff policy, retries, and returns success to caller instead of propagating initial error.
